learner_goal: I want to explain why vaccines work.
launch_attempt: Vaccines show the immune system a safer preview so it can react faster later.
cold_attempt: I'm not sure, but I think a safe preview leaves memory cells that respond faster later.
repair: Memory cells preserve the faster response after the safe antigen preview.
spaced_attempt: A vaccine safely presents antigen, memory cells remain, and those cells respond faster during a later infection.
